title: incarvillea lutea
intro: Incarvillea lutea, also known as the Yellow Incarvillea, is a flowering plant species in the bignonia family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible roots. The medicinal properties of Incarvillea lutea include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and aid in digestion. Incarvillea lutea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and its roots can be dried and used as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
